topic: Importing Existing Student Data, Dues, and Concessions
summary: This topic outlines the process and requirements for importing data for existing students, including their academic records, outstanding dues from previous sessions, and any applicable concessions, to ensure a smooth transition and enable fee collection in the new ERP system.
steps: Provide the ERP team with data for all existing (old) students in the specified Excel format.
Ensure the student data includes unique identifiers such as Admission Number and University Roll Number.
Submit data for any outstanding dues (pending amounts) from previous academic sessions.
Provide data for all existing student concessions that need to be carried forward into the new system.
The ERP team will then import the provided data into the system.
business_rules: Each student's registration number (admission number) must be unique.
Both the admission number and university roll number should be included in the student data.
New admissions will be assigned a new series of registration numbers.
important_notes: Importing accurate old student data is crucial for initiating fee collection and maintaining historical records.
Detailed data on previous dues is necessary for accurate financial reconciliation.
Explicitly sharing concession data for old students is required if these concessions are to continue in the new ERP.
The ERP team will provide sample Excel formats to guide data submission for students, transport, and hostel.
questions: How do we import our existing student data into the new ERP system?
What specific information is required for importing old student records?
How should we provide data for outstanding dues from previous academic years?
Will existing student concessions be automatically carried forward after data import?
What are the unique identifiers needed for student data during the import process?
